title: WorldCoordinateEvent
description: Canonical reference frame for any spacetime event across the emperor's portfolio. WGS84 geodetic position + UTC timestamp. Adopted by the Mathematician in Session 3; ruled binding in Session 4.

known_failure_modes: Confusing WGS84 geodetic latitude with geocentric latitude for satellite subpoints (differ up to ~11 arcmin).
Reporting altitude without frame (ellipsoid height vs mean-sea-level differ by tens of meters).
Dropping the uncertainty band because 'the source didn't include one' — physics does not permit unitless certainty.

schema_version.description: Semver. Major bump on breaking change; minor on additive; patch on cosmetic.

lat_deg.description: Geodetic latitude in degrees (WGS84 by default). Positive north.

alt_m.description: Altitude in meters. Reference frame named by 'frame' below.

frame.description: Reference frame for the altitude value. WGS84_ellipsoid is the default and preferred frame.

uncertainty_m.description: 1-sigma spherical position uncertainty in meters. Required for measured positions; omit for user-defined.

timestamp_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Must include the 'Z' suffix or explicit +00:00 offset. Local times are rejected.

timestamp_uncertainty_s.description: 1-sigma timing uncertainty in seconds. TLE-derived positions should carry the epoch age here in seconds.

source.description: Provenance chain. Required when the event was ingested from an external source.

primary.description: The authoritative record. e.g. 'USSPACECOM catalog', 'GOGPT Jan 2026 release'.

aggregator.description: Any intermediary. e.g. 'n2yo.com', 'CelesTrak'. Must be named when the primary was not fetched directly.

metadata.description: Event-type-specific payload. Structure not enforced by this schema.
